---
title: "Batch Range Inference"
---

## Overview

Batch range inference runs a model independently on every frame in a
user-specified range `[start_frame, end_frame]`. Unlike [recurrent
inference](inference_controller.qmd#recurrent-sequence) (where each frame's
output feeds into the next), batch range inference treats each frame as a
standalone prediction вЂ” no state is carried between frames.

Runs execute on a background `QThread` so the UI stays responsive during long
jobs. Decoded geometry (masks, points, lines) appears progressively in the
view as frames complete; feature vectors are flushed to `TensorData` when the
run finishes.

Orchestration lives in [`InferenceController`](inference_controller.qmd).
Model batch-size constraints are documented in
[Batch Mode Guide](../../../DeepLearning/batch_mode_guide.qmd).

## User workflow

1. Configure model and slot bindings as for single-frame inference.
2. Click **в–¶в–¶ Run Batch** in the Properties panel.
3. Choose a mode in the dialog:
   - **Manual range** вЂ” enter start and end frame indices.
   - **From interval series** вЂ” select a `DigitalIntervalSeries` key; each
     interval is processed sequentially with unified progress.
4. The View widget progress bar shows per-frame progress.
5. On completion or cancellation, results are written to DataManager at their
   respective frame indices.

While a batch runs:

- **Run Batch** becomes **Cancel Batch**.
- **Run Single** and **Run Recurrent** are disabled.
- The Qt event loop stays free (no `processEvents()` hacks).

## Architecture

```
DeepLearningPropertiesWidget::_onRunBatch()
    в†’ InferenceController::runBatch() / runBatchIntervals()
        в†’ BatchInferenceWorker / MultiIntervalBatchWorker (QThread)
            в†’ SlotAssembler::runBatchRangeOffline()
                в†’ ResultCallback в†’ WriteReservation::push()
        в†’ ResultProcessor (QTimer 200 ms) в†’ WriteReservation::drain()
            в†’ acceptResults() в†’ DataManager::addAtTime()
```

| Component | Header | Role |
|-----------|--------|------|
| `InferenceController` | `Inference/InferenceController.hpp` | Owns worker lifecycle, media clones, cancel flag |
| `BatchInferenceWorker` | `Inference/BatchInferenceWorker.hpp` | Single contiguous frame range |
| `MultiIntervalBatchWorker` | `Inference/MultiIntervalBatchWorker.hpp` | Multiple intervals, cumulative progress |
| `WriteReservation` | `Inference/WriteReservation.hpp` | Thread-safe buffer between worker and main thread |
| `ResultProcessor` | `Inference/ResultProcessor.hpp` | Timer-driven merge into DataManager |
| `BatchInferenceResult` | `Inference/BatchInferenceResult.hpp` | `FrameResult`, `DecodedOutputVariant`, error status |
| `SlotAssembler` | `Core/SlotAssembler.hpp` | `runBatchRangeOffline()` inference loop |
| `MediaOverrides` | `Core/MediaOverrides.hpp` | `data_key в†’ MediaData` map for thread-safe reads |

Workers are dedicated header/`.cpp` pairs (not anonymous namespaces) so
AUTOMOC can generate meta-object code without `.moc` includes in `.cpp` files.

## SlotAssembler offline API

`SlotAssembler::runBatchRangeOffline()` is the core per-frame loop. It is
identical to the synchronous `runBatchRange()` except:

- **`MediaOverrides`** вЂ” worker reads media through cloned `VideoData` instances
  keyed by `data_key`, avoiding FFmpeg decoder contention with the UI thread.
- **No direct DataManager writes** вЂ” decoded outputs are returned as
  `FrameResult` entries (`DecodedOutputVariant` geometry or feature vector)
  instead of calling `addAtTime()` on the worker thread.
- **Cancellation** вЂ” polls `std::atomic<bool> const & cancel_requested` before
  each batch chunk; returns partial success on early exit.
- **`batch_size`** вЂ” number of frames per forward pass (last chunk may be
  smaller). See [Batch Mode Guide](../../../DeepLearning/batch_mode_guide.qmd).

Optional callbacks:

```cpp
using ProgressCallback = std::function<void(int current_frame, int total_frames)>;
using ResultCallback   = std::function<void(std::vector<FrameResult>)>;
```

When a `ResultCallback` is provided, results are pushed per chunk via the
callback and **not** accumulated in `BatchInferenceResult::results`. Workers
always pass a callback that forwards to `WriteReservation::push()`.

`runBatchRange()` (synchronous, writes directly to DataManager) remains in
`SlotAssembler` for tests and programmatic use; the widget always uses the
offline path through `InferenceController`.

## Background threading pattern

The implementation follows the **thread confinement + merge** model from the
[Concurrency Architecture](../../../DataManager/concurrency_architecture.qmd):

1. **DataManager stays single-threaded.** All writes happen on the main (Qt) thread.
2. **The worker uses private media.** Cloned `VideoData` gives the worker its
   own FFmpeg decoder. Non-video `MediaData` is shared (stateless reads).
   Static memory-frame sources that reference video are cloned the same way.
3. **Results merge on the main thread.** `ResultProcessor` runs a `QTimer`
   (200 ms) that drains `WriteReservation` and writes geometry via
   `addAtTime(..., NotifyObservers::No)`, then calls `notifyObservers()` once
   per affected key.
4. **Qt signals cross threads.** `progressChanged` and completion signals use
   `Qt::QueuedConnection` automatically.

```
Worker thread                    Main thread
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ                    в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
runBatchRangeOffline()
  decode chunk в”Ђв”Ђв†’ push(results)
                                 QTimer (200 ms) в”Ђв”Ђв†’ drain()
  decode chunk в”Ђв”Ђв†’ push(results)                     в”њв”Ђ acceptResults()
                                                     в”‚    в””в”Ђ addAtTime(...)
                                                     в””в”Ђ notifyObservers()
QThread::finished в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв†’ stopMergeTimer()
                                    в”њв”Ђ final drain()
                                    в””в”Ђ flushFeatureVectors()
```

### WriteReservation

Thread-safe accumulation buffer. The worker calls `push()` after each decoded
chunk; `ResultProcessor` calls `drain()` on timer ticks and on worker
completion. A `std::mutex` protects only the pending vector вЂ” DataManager is
never touched under the lock.

### ResultProcessor

Owns the merge timer and writes drained results into DataManager:

- **Geometry** (`Mask2D`, `Point2D<float>`, `Line2D`) вЂ” written immediately
  via `addAtTime(..., NotifyObservers::No)`, then `notifyObservers()` per key.
- **Feature vectors** (`std::vector<float>`) вЂ” accumulated in
  `_pending_feature_rows` and flushed to `TensorData` via `upsertRows()` in
  `flushFeatureVectors()` when the worker finishes.

`InferenceController` calls `clear()` at batch start, `startMergeTimer()` when
the worker launches, and `stopMergeTimer()` + `flushFeatureVectors()` in the
`QThread::finished` handler.

### Why a separate VideoData?

`VideoData` wraps a stateful FFmpeg decoder (seek position, codec context,
frame buffer). Two threads cannot read from the same instance without corrupting
each other's state. `InferenceController` clones `VideoData` at the same file
path for every image-encoder binding and static memory-frame video source.

This aligns with `ConcurrencyTraits<VideoData>::supports_cheap_clone = true`.

## Cancellation

`InferenceController::cancel()` sets a shared `std::atomic<bool>` flag read by
`runBatchRangeOffline()` between batch chunks. Clicking **Cancel Batch** (or
starting a new run while one is active) triggers this path. Partial results
already pushed to `WriteReservation` are merged on the next timer tick and in
the final drain.

The destructor also sets the cancel flag and waits for the worker to exit
cleanly.

## Progress reporting

| Path | Signal chain |
|------|--------------|
| Single range | `BatchInferenceWorker::progressChanged` в†’ `InferenceController::batchProgressChanged` в†’ `DeepLearningPropertiesWidget` в†’ `DeepLearningViewWidget` |
| Multi-interval | `MultiIntervalBatchWorker` reports cumulative progress across all intervals |

Recurrent inference runs synchronously on the GUI thread and uses
`recurrentProgressChanged` instead; see
[InferenceController](inference_controller.qmd).

## Generalizing to other components

The same write-reservation + timer merge pattern applies to any long-running
computation that must keep DataManager on the main thread:

1. Share a `WriteReservation` between worker and main thread.
2. Push results from the worker via callback.
3. Drain periodically with `QTimer` on the main thread.
4. Perform a final drain on `QThread::finished`.

See [Concurrency Architecture](../../../DataManager/concurrency_architecture.qmd)
for the full design.

## See also

- [InferenceController](inference_controller.qmd) вЂ” public API, signals, recurrent path
- [Batch Mode Guide](../../../DeepLearning/batch_mode_guide.qmd) вЂ” model batch-size constraints
- [Concurrency Architecture](../../../DataManager/concurrency_architecture.qmd) вЂ” merge pattern rationale
